body:
Trainer Resource Hub

Welcome to the National Foster Parent Training Resources space. This is a read-only library for certified PRIDE and pre-service trainers. Start here for facilitation guides, compliance checklists, and recruitment resources. Use the left navigation or search (CQL) to find materials by module or topic.

  • Facilitation guides by module
  • Trauma-informed practice
  • Recruitment & retention
  • Licensing & compliance
title: Trainer Resource Hub

body:
PRIDE Module Facilitation Guide

Session-by-session facilitation notes for the ten-session PRIDE pre-service model. Each module includes learning objectives, suggested timing, discussion prompts, and home-practice assignments. Trainers may adapt activities to their cohort but should preserve the core competencies and the connecting/visiting components.

  • Module 1: Connecting with PRIDE
  • Module 3: Meeting developmental needs & loss
  • Module 7: Continuing relationships
title: PRIDE Module Facilitation Guide

body:
Trauma-Informed Training Techniques

Guidance for facilitating sensitive material with prospective resource parents. Covers grounding activities, recognizing when a participant is activated, pacing difficult content (loss, abuse dynamics, the impact of separation), and creating psychological safety in the training room. Includes a list of co-regulation strategies trainers can model.
title: Trauma-Informed Training Techniques

body:
Recruiting Foster Parents in Faith Communities

Field-tested approaches for partnering with congregations to recruit and support resource families. Includes a sample information-night agenda, talking points for clergy, and tips for honoring a community's culture while presenting the realities of fostering. Emphasizes follow-up within 72 hours of an inquiry.
title: Recruiting Foster Parents in Faith Communities

body:
Pre-Service Training Compliance Checklist

Documentation trainers must complete for each cohort: attendance logs, hours certification (most jurisdictions require 27-30 pre-service hours), participant evaluations, and CPR/First Aid sign-off where applicable. Use this checklist before submitting a cohort roster to the licensing agency.

  • Attendance per session
  • Total contact hours
  • Evaluation forms collected
  • Certificates issued
title: Pre-Service Training Compliance Checklist

body:
Working with Kinship Caregivers

Adapting pre-service content for relative and kinship caregivers, who often enter the system in crisis and on short notice. Covers the differences in their training needs, navigating family loyalty and grief, and connecting them to kinship navigator programs and subsidized guardianship resources.
title: Working with Kinship Caregivers

body:
Co-Facilitation & Trainer Self-Care

Best practices for pairing trainers, dividing modules, and debriefing after emotionally heavy sessions. Includes a short reflection protocol for trainers and reminders about secondary traumatic stress. Strongly recommended for trainers who also carry a caseload.
title: Co-Facilitation & Trainer Self-Care

body:
Building the Cohort Workbook

Template and printing guidance for assembling a participant workbook from the PRIDE materials: handout sequencing, fillable home-practice pages, a resource directory page, and a graduation/next-steps section. Includes accessible-format and reprint recommendations.
title: Building the Cohort Workbook
